result.vlm_summary:
page_state:
Google Flights date picker modal is open for a round trip. Visible months are February 2026 and March 2026, with left/right month navigation arrows and a blue Done button. A red warning banner says “You are performing actions too quickly. Please slow down and try this action again.”
task_relevant_visible_text:
“Round trip”, “Departure”, “Return”, “February 2026”, “March 2026”, “Showing prices in USD for 7 day trips”, “Done”, “You are performing actions too quickly. Please slow down and try this action again.”
selected_candidate:
No date selected yet; no visible round-trip candidate matching the target dates.
constraint_evidence:
- Round-trip flight: supported (visible “Round trip”)
- Depart from Los Angeles: not_visible (origin field not visible in this screenshot)
- Arrive in Zurich: not_visible (destination field not visible in this screenshot)
- Departing on April 22, 2026: not_visible (April 2026 not currently visible)
- Returning on May 1, 2026: not_visible (May 2026 not currently visible)
- Flight search on Google Flights: supported (title/url context and visible Google Flights UI)
action_target:
Use the right month navigation arrow to advance the calendar toward April/May 2026, despite the rate-limit warning.
success_signals:
April 2026 and May 2026 become visible; target departure/return dates can be selected; date picker updates accordingly.
failure_signals:
No month change occurs; warning persists; wrong dates/months selected; modal closes without settin

result.vlm_summary:
page_state:
Google Flights results page showing round-trip search from Los Angeles to Zürich with dates Wed, Apr 22 and Fri, May 1; results list visible.
task_relevant_visible_text:
Round trip; Los Angeles; Zürich; Wed, Apr 22; Fri, May 1; Top departing flights; Cheapest from $730; SAS $791 round trip; SWISS $879 round trip; Air Canada $886 round trip.
selected_candidate:
No specific flight selected; top visible candidate is SAS round trip for $791.
constraint_evidence:
- Round-trip flight: supported (visible “Round trip” and URL context).
- Departing from Los Angeles: supported (origin field shows Los Angeles).
- Arriving in Zurich: supported (destination field shows Zürich).
- Departing on April 22, 2026: supported (date field shows Wed, Apr 22; URL context includes 2026-04-22).
- Returning on May 1, 2026: supported (date field shows Fri, May 1; URL context includes 2026-05-01).
- Need a flight search result/selection: supported (results list visible).
- Price qualifier: supported for visible results ($730 cheapest, $791/$879/$886 round trip).
- Airline/category details: supported for visible results (SAS, SWISS, Air Canada; nonstop/1 stop).
action_target:
Send exit message to user: [EXIT]
success_signals:
Search criteria are already set correctly and matching results are visible.
failure_signals:
No final booking/selection made; no explicit confirmation of a chosen itinerary.
uncertainty:
Whether the task expects selecting a specific flight versus simply confirming the sea

args.task: navigate: Search for a round-trip flight from Los Angeles to Zurich, departing on April 22, 2026, and returning on May 1, 2026.

evidence.claim: The run ends on a Google Flights results page with the target route and dates visible: Los Angeles to Zürich, Wed, Apr 22, returning Fri, May 1.
The high-detail inspection of the final screenshot shows the task constraints are satisfied and matching results are visible, including the round-trip route and dates.
A prior calendar interaction showed the agent had navigated into the date picker and received a rate-limit warning while moving toward April/May 2026, but this did not prevent eventual success.
No failure evidence is visible in the final outcome; the trace reaches the requested search results page rather than stalling or selecting the wrong route/date.

suggested_followups.label: Inspect final result page
suggested_followups.message: Show me the final Google Flights results page details and the visible fares.

suggested_followups.label: Compare with step 11
suggested_followups.message: Compare the rate-limit warning at step 11 with the successful final state.
